scenario_results.reason: The specialist resolved the workplace request with work-order-backed room-readiness status, service impact, and safe next-step communication.
The specialist stopped on missing facts instead of improvising around unknown location, safety, permit, and record context.
The unsupported policy exception was refused and routed to the correct approval owner.
Repeat-failure history triggered escalation instead of another unsupported temporary service workaround.
The lane converted a workplace-service communication into a clear cross-lane handoff with owner and blocker context.
Licensed trade and code-interpretation authority were refused and routed outward.
The specialist blocked service commitment until permit, punch-list, and site-readiness prerequisites were explicit.
Inspection and closeout blockers prevented premature turnover or completion messaging.
A changed safety posture correctly halted normal room-ready assurances and forced escalation.
Conflicting tenant and public occupancy rules were escalated instead of synthesized into unsupported authority.
Tenant-specific vocabulary was adapted while preserving public-baseline evidence and boundary controls.
Facilities and workplace terminology was normalized into authoritative artifact handling rather than generic office-support language.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all service-support-and-resolution abilities, and both construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
